gold.rationale: 样本文本的核心事实与现有公开信息总体一致：新格伦NG-3确实完成起飞，问题主要出在上面级，BlueBird 7未进入预定轨道，FAA也启动了相关调查程序。偏差主要在于文中对一级阶段“关键验证”“里程碑意义”等表述略偏积极，轻度放大了任务中部分技术进展的正面意义，但不足以改变整体事实判断。

atoms: A white rocket is ascending vertically into a grey sky with a bright exhaust plume.
Two tall metal lattice launch towers flank the rocket on either side.
A large plume of white smoke and clouds is visible behind the rocket.
A water tower stands in the background on the right side.
Silhouettes of trees and vegetation are visible at the bottom of the frame.
A white rocket is shown ascending diagonally against a plain grey sky.
A trail of exhaust smoke extends from the rocket's base downwards.
The rocket features a black section near the engine and a black band around the body.
A white rocket is ascending vertically with a visible exhaust plume.
A digital overlay at the bottom displays telemetry data including 'NEW GLENN VELOCITY', 'NEW GLENN ALTITUDE', and 'T+00:00:29'.
The text 'LIFTOFF' is visible on the timeline overlay.
The text 'MAX Q' is visible on the timeline overlay.
The rocket body has black markings or panels.
An illustration depicts a satellite with large solar panels orbiting Earth.
The Earth shows the curvature of the horizon with visible landmasses and clouds.
The sun is shining brightly on the right side of the Earth.
Additional smaller satellite components or debris are shown floating in space to the right.
An infographic titled 'NEW GLENN-3 FLIGHT PROFILE' shows a rocket trajectory.
The trajectory includes labels for 'LIFTOFF', 'ASCENT', 'STAGE SEPARATION', 'FAIRING JETTISON', 'GS2 BURN', and 'SPACECRAFT SEPARATION'.
A map of the world shows the flight path over the Atlantic Ocean.
Text on the right lists 'Mission Orbital Parameters' including 'APOGEE: 460 KM', 'PERIGEE: 460 KM', and 'INCLINATION: 49.4°'.
The launch site is identified as 'LC-36, FL' and landing site as 'JACKLYN'.
Icons of the launch tower and landing pad are shown at the bottom.

retrieved_evidence.text: finally saw a New Glenn launch, a truly incredible machine

AST SpaceMobile规划中的BlueBird系列卫星旨在构建面向移动终端的直接卫星通信网络，通过大型相控阵天线实现与普通手机的直连服务。BlueBird 7被视为该系列的重要节点卫星，其设计围绕大面积展开结构、电源管理与宽带通信载荷展开。根据公司介绍，此次与蓝色起源“新格伦”合作发射，原计划将BlueBird 7送入特定轨道高度和倾角，以验证商业网络能力，但由于上面级异常导致轨道偏离，给后续星座部署带来不确定性。
蓝色起源新格伦火箭本周从佛罗里达发射场按计划起飞，现场转播画面显示，火箭在预定时间窗口内顺利点火，拔地升空并完成初始上升段飞行。观测视频中可见火焰与尾迹稳定，地面控制中心也在发射后不久宣布各系统状态“表现正常”。根据初步技术通报，起飞和早期上升阶段未出现明显异常，相关飞行参数有待后续公开。
此次“新格伦”飞行过程中，火箭在爬升阶段依次完成姿态控制、分段推进和穿越最大动压点等关键环节，外界普遍认为早期飞行段表现体现出蓝色起源在大推力发动机与结构设计上的工程积累。根据相关报道，一级火箭按照回收目标执行了轨迹控制和减速程序，但真正影响任务整体评价的，是分离后的上面级推进与轨道插入表现未达预期，进而牵连BlueBird 7载荷的运营部署。
美国私营航天企业蓝色起源近日执行了其重型运载火箭“新格伦”的最新一次飞行任务，这是该型火箭继首飞后开展的第三次轨道发射活动。业内人士普遍将此次飞行称作“新格伦第三次任务”，认为其在技术验证和商业运载布局方面具有节点意义。火箭从佛罗里达海岸发射，主要用于验证后续高密度发射节奏的能力，相关详细数据仍在汇总分析中。
据参与项目的工程人员介绍，此次新格伦火箭执行的是与商业通信公司AST SpaceMobile合作的运载任务，主要载荷为一颗名为BlueBird 7的通信卫星。该卫星被视为相关公司规划中大型卫星互联网星座的重要组成部分，计划用于开展大范围移动通信服务验证。发射合作被认为是蓝色起源拓展商业客户和大型卫星托运市场的一次重要尝试。

网络直播与航天追踪平台记录了“新格伦”在各飞行阶段的速度、高度与轨迹数据，凸显此次任务是一场完整的轨道发射尝试而非单纯的演示飞行。相关数据表明，火箭在初始推进和一级分离环节接近设计指标，任务成败的关键节点集中在上面级入轨段的推力输出与控制表现。正是在这一阶段出现的异常，使得AST SpaceMobile计划中的BlueBird 7大规模卫星通信部署无法按原定轨道实施，成为后续调查与行业讨论的焦点。
多名了解情况的业内人士透露，围绕这次新格伦任务的讨论焦点，并非集中在能否成功点火发射，而是上面级在入轨阶段出现疑似故障或状态异常，导致所携带的BlueBird 7卫星未能按照原定方案进入目标轨道。有关人士称，具体异常机理和影响范围尚在技术排查中，后续可能需要通过轨道机动或其他方式，评估载荷任务的可持续性。
蓝色起源研制的“新格伦”（New Glenn）重型运载火箭在此次任务中按计划从佛罗里达州海岸附近的发射设施起飞，点火后尾焰清晰可见，顺利脱离发射台并进入上升轨道。业内分析指出，起飞与一级飞行阶段的成功展示了其液氧甲烷动力与地面发射系统的成熟度，但核心争议在于后续上面级异常导致AST SpaceMobile的BlueBird 7未能进入预定轨道，从而引发美国联邦航空管理局对整体任务可靠性的关注。

#Breaking 
FAA probes #Jeff_Bezos; Blue Origin’s ‘New Glenn 3’ rocket failure left ASTS BlueBird 7 in the wrong orbit; flights are paused pending approval before resuming.

#Blue_Origin, the space launch company founded by Jeff Bezos, achieved a successful recovery of its first reused orbital-class rocket, but the payload it placed into space ended up in the wrong location.
与“新格伦”相关的发射设施位于美国东海岸的空间港区域，该地区集中了多家商业与政府航天发射活动，形成较为完善的地面基础设施和空域协调机制。根据地方政府和航天产业发展规划，蓝色起源的长期布局包括扩大发动机测试能力、完善一级回收后处理流程以及为客户提供更高频次的发射服务。此次因上面级问题引发的FAA调查，使得这些规划不得不重新考虑安全冗余和认证要求，也促使区域航天产业链重新审视技术和管理标准。

The FAA has ordered a mishap investigation into Blue Origin’s New Glenn 3 upper-stage failure after $ASTS BlueBird 7 was not placed into the intended orbit.

Blue Origin will need FAA approval on corrective actions before New Glenn can return to flight.
美国联邦航空管理局相关负责人近日表示，已依法启动针对新格伦本次飞行任务的事故或异常调查程序。据介绍，该部门在接到运载火箭入轨阶段出现问题的通报后，要求运营方提交详细飞行数据和初步技术分析报告，以便评估事件对公共安全和后续飞行许可的影响。相关调查工作仍处于早期阶段，后续将视情况发布进一步说明。
据接近监管流程的消息人士透露，在蓝色起源就新格伦上面级异常提出纠正措施方案并获得联邦航空管理局批准之前，该型火箭后续发射将受到不同程度限制。业内分析认为，这意味着新格伦短期内难以按原计划快速提升发射频率，相关公司需要在技术整改、验证试验和监管协调等方面投入更多时间与资源，以满足安全和合规要求。
资料显示，蓝色起源由亚马逊创始人贝索斯创办，被视为美国商业航天领域的重要参与者之一。其中，新格伦重型运载火箭项目长期被该公司视为实现大规模近地轨道和更远空间运输能力的核心平台。航天观察人士指出，尽管本次任务结果存在争议，但仍被看作该公司迈向更高运载能力和更复杂任务配置过程中的重要一步，后续发展动向备受关注。
在对本次新格伦任务的评估中，多名航天专家指出，火箭一级推进和结构系统总体表现积极，助推段的既定任务目标据称取得一定进展，包括与回收相关的若干技术验证目标。但专家同时强调，由于上面级在入轨段出现问题导致主要载荷难以完全按照原计划执行，因此不能简单将此次发射描述为“任务圆满成功”，而应将一级表现与整体任务结果并列考量。

AST SpaceMobile与多家移动运营商合作，计划通过BlueBird星座提供全球范围的宽带直连服务，其商业模式依托卫星与现有蜂窝网络的互补。在BlueBird 7未进入预定轨道的背景下，分析人士认为公司需要重新评估星座部署时间表和资本开支安排。根据公开财报，AST SpaceMobile对“新格伦”等新一代运载工具寄予厚望，此次发射结果不仅关系单颗卫星的命运，也牵动与融资方、合作运营商之间的预期管理。
美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）作为负责商业航天发射许可与安全监管的机构，在“新格伦”任务出现上面级异常后，已依据相关法规要求蓝色起源提交详细事件报告并展开事故调查。公开资料显示，FAA通常会评估系统设计、操作程序与潜在风险，对纠正措施提出要求，只有在确认问题得到有效整改后，才会为后续发射活动重新签发或恢复许可。因此，“新格伦”火箭的复飞节奏将受到调查进展与监管审批的直接影响。

text_analysis.alignment_reasoning: The post accurately reports the event details and technical findings but uses dramatic framing and definitive conclusions that exceed the cautious tone of the evidence.
text_analysis.t2_evidence: The post states 'BlueBird 7... has encountered obvious setbacks' and 'the task has always carried an unbridgeable gap', whereas evidence [5] and [11] only describe the satellite as being in a 'non-standard orbit' and notes that the outcome 'cannot be simply described as a complete task success' without confirming the specific 'setback' or 'gap' language.
t2_problematic_subclaims: The post claims the task 'encountered obvious setbacks' (遭遇明显挫折), which is a stronger judgment than the evidence's 'non-standard orbit' (非标轨道).

The post concludes the task 'always carries an unbridgeable gap' (始终带着一个绕不过去的缺口), whereas evidence [11] suggests the result is 'controversial' and requires 'parallel consideration' of the first stage's success.
text_analysis.global_assessment: The post is factually grounded but employs emotionally charged language and definitive conclusions that slightly overstate the ambiguity present in the official reports.

images.scene_description: A New Glenn rocket is shown ascending vertically from a launchpad, flanked by service towers, with a large plume of smoke and fire at the base.
images.explanation: The image depicts the launch phase of the New Glenn rocket, consistent with the post's claim and the ground-truth evidence.

images.scene_description: A rocket is seen ascending into a grey sky, leaving a trail of exhaust, appearing to be in the ascent phase.
images.explanation: The image shows the rocket ascending, which aligns with the post's description of the flight trajectory.

images.explanation: The image displays specific telemetry data consistent with the early phase of the New Glenn launch described in the text.

images.scene_description: A highly stylized, computer-generated illustration of a satellite with large solar panels orbiting Earth, presented as a poster.
images.v1_evidence: The image has the visual characteristics of a high-fidelity AI-generated press-style poster or marketing illustration (stylized gradients, perfect composition, no candid imperfections) rather than a real photograph.
images.explanation: While the subject matter matches the text, the image style is clearly synthetic/illustrative, fitting the V1 definition of a press-style image that would not arise from candid photography.

images.v1_evidence: The image exhibits the visual signature of a high-fidelity AI-generated press-style poster (uniformly sharp, perfectly composed, glossy render) rather than a real photograph.
images.explanation: The image is a synthetic illustration matching the satellite description, flagged as V1 due to its non-photographic, poster-like aesthetic.

images.scene_description: A highly stylized, computer-generated illustration of a satellite with large solar panels orbiting Earth, presented as a poster.
images.v1_evidence: The image displays the visual traits of a high-fidelity AI-generated press-style poster (stylized gradients, flawless lighting, commercial render look) inconsistent with real-world photography.
images.explanation: The image is a synthetic illustration matching the satellite description, flagged as V1 due to its non-photographic, poster-like aesthetic.

image_analysis.global_notes: Images 4, 5, and 6 are synthetic illustrations used to depict the satellite, while images 1, 2, and 3 are real photos or telemetry data.

judge.level3_rationale: 尽管文本准确描述了新格伦3号任务的技术细节和后果，但文中引用的卫星和飞行剖面图像均为高保真AI生成插图而非真实拍摄照片，且文本对任务失败的定性略显夸张，导致图文结合的整体可信度下降。
judge.reasoning_trace: Textual analysis confirms T2 distortion in framing; Visual analysis confirms V1 synthetic content in images 4-6 and C2 contextual mismatch (illustration vs. event). L2 count (3) + V1 presence mandates Mostly False.
judge.core_claim: The post describes the New Glenn 3 launch on April 19, 2026, where the first stage performed well but the upper stage failed to place the BlueBird 7 satellite into the intended orbit, triggering an FAA investigation.
key_subclaims: The launch occurred on April 19, 2026, at LC-36, Florida.
The first stage performed positively, with successful recovery verification.
The upper stage failed to place BlueBird 7 into the intended orbit.
The FAA has ordered an investigation and requires corrective measures before the rocket can return to flight.
The post images depict the launch and the satellite in orbit.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The launch occurred on April 19, 2026, at LC-36, Florida.
subclaim_findings.note: Confirmed by news snippets and image 5 infographic.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The first stage performed positively, with successful recovery verification.
subclaim_findings.note: Confirmed by evidence stating first stage performance was positive and recovery targets were met.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The upper stage failed to place BlueBird 7 into the intended orbit.
subclaim_findings.note: Confirmed by evidence stating the satellite was not placed into the intended orbit.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The FAA has ordered an investigation and requires corrective measures before the rocket can return to flight.
subclaim_findings.note: Confirmed by evidence regarding FAA mishap investigation and approval requirements.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The post images depict the launch and the satellite in orbit.
subclaim_findings.note: Images 1-3 show the launch; Images 4-6 are illustrative CGI of the satellite, not actual photos of the specific mission event.

judge.verdict_reasoning: The post accurately reports the event details and technical findings but uses dramatic framing that slightly overstates the severity (T2). Crucially, the post includes AI-generated illustrations (V1) of the satellite and flight profile to support the narrative, which are not actual photos of the event (C2). The presence of V1 and C2 labels elevates the verdict to Mostly False.

image_findings.explanation: A candid photo of the New Glenn rocket launch at LC-36, consistent with the text description of the launch event.
A photo of the rocket ascending, consistent with the text description of the ascent phase.
A telemetry screenshot showing the rocket at liftoff, consistent with the text description of the launch telemetry.
A high-fidelity AI-generated illustration of a satellite in orbit, not a candid photo of the actual BlueBird 7 satellite from the specific mission.
A high-fidelity AI-generated infographic illustrating the flight profile, not a candid photo of the actual mission trajectory.
A high-fidelity AI-generated illustration of a satellite in orbit, identical in style to image 4, not a candid photo of the actual BlueBird 7 satellite.
